content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>那你幫我都修正</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-03</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
檢查完成。結論先說：**tsc 通過、commit 內容與訊息大致相符，但三個 P0 模組中只有 Ingest Ripple 真的接上線，且 Query Auto-save 內部有一個會讓功能永遠不觸發的 bug**。Roadmap HTML 把三項都標「✅ 完成」與實際狀態不符。

## 嚴重問題

**1. `query-auto-save.ts` 讀 topics.json 的方式是錯的，功能永遠不會觸發**（`src/query-auto-save.ts:80-90`）

`loadTopicNames()` 直接 `JSON.parse(readFileSync(topicsPath))`，但這個 repo 的 topics.json 是帶 `//` 標頭註解的 JSONC——`topic-rules.ts:119-126` 就是特地先 strip 註解才能 parse。這裡沒 strip，parse 會 throw 然後回空陣列。而且就算 parse 成功，欄位名也錯了：實際 schema 是 `{topic, keywords}`（見 `topic-rules.ts:72`），這裡讀的是 `t.name`。兩個錯任一個都導致 topic 清單永遠是空的 → `detectTopics` 永遠匹配 0 個 → 永遠達不到 `MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY = 3` → `shouldSave` 永遠 false。建議修法是直接重用 `getTopicRules()`（順便能拿到 keywords 做更準的匹配，比現在拆 kebab-case 猜關鍵字好）。

**2. `query-auto-save.ts` 和 `activity-log.ts` 完全沒有接線（dead code）**

全 repo grep 過，`evaluateForAutoSave` / `recordAutoSaveCandidate` / `queryActivity` / `getActivitySummary` 沒有任何 caller——`run-prompt.ts` 在這個 commit 沒被改到。Roadmap 寫「evaluateForAutoSave() 供 run-prompt 完成後評估」，但 run-prompt 並沒有呼叫它。commit message 說的「自動偵測優質回覆並存為 wiki 候選」目前不會發生。

## 中等問題

**3. Ingest Ripple 只接了生產端，沒接消費端**。`onFactIngested` 確實掛進 `mcp-memory.ts` 的 `remember()`（這條線是對的），但 `getRippleStatus()` / `clearProcessedRipples()` 沒人呼叫，`/dream` 的 wikisync 步驟不讀 `wiki-ripple.jsonl`——log 只寫不讀。有 200 筆 trim 上限所以不會爆量，但目前是寫心酸的。

**4. shard 檔名是用猜的**（`src/ingest-ripple.ts:134-137`）。`getShardFactIds` 自己拼 `${topic}.md`，沒重用 `facts-store.ts:59` 的 `topicToFilename()`（會 lowercase + 空白轉 dash + 濾特殊字元）。topic 若含大寫或空白就找不到 shard → `pendingCount` 算成 0 → 門檻永遠不達。純 kebab-case topic 沒事，但應該重用正式函式。

**5. `mcp-memory.ts:116` 的 factId 格式不對且目前無用**。`match(...)?.[0]` 取到的是含中括號的 `[f_xxxxxx]`，而 wiki sources frontmatter 用的是 `f_xxxxxx`。目前無害——因為 `onFactIngested` 的 `factId` 參數宣告了但函式內根本沒用到——但哪天真的用上就會 mismatch。要嘛改取 `m[1]` 加前綴（或直接用現成的 `extractFactId()`），要嘛把這個死參數拿掉。

## 通過的部分

- `npx tsc --noEmit` 通過
- 三個 log 路徑（`hit-log.jsonl` / `events.jsonl` / `observations.jsonl`）與各寫入端一致；`facts_Topic` 目錄命名正確
- wiki index 解析的 `- [[slug]] — 描述` 格式與 `wiki-retrieval.ts` 一致
- `onFactIngested` 全程 try-catch、fire-and-forget，不會影響 `remember()` 主路徑——設計正確
- `.gitignore` 加 `output/` 沒問題

## 建議

commit 不用 revert（hook 那條線是好的、也不會壞現有功能），但 roadmap 的「✅ 全部完成」建議改成「程式碼落地、接線待補」，或直接補三件事：修 `loadTopicNames`（問題 1）、把 `evaluateForAutoSave` 接進 run-prompt 完成點、讓 `/dream` wikisync 讀 ripple log 和 auto-save 候選。需要我直接修的話說一聲。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

**4. shard 檔名是用猜的**（`src/ingest-ripple.ts:134-137`）。`getShardFactIds` 自己拼 `${topic}.md`，沒重用 `facts-store.ts:59` 的 `topicToFilename()`（會 lowercase + 空白轉 dash + 濾特殊字元）。topic 若含大寫或空白就找不到 shard → `pendingCount` 算成 0 → 門檻永遠不達。純 kebab-case topic 沒事，但應該重用正式函式。

**5. `mcp-memory.ts:116` 的 factId 格式不對且目前無用**。`match(...)?.[0]` 取到的是含中括號的 `[f_xxxxxx]`，而 wiki sources frontmatter 用的是 `f_xxxxxx`。目前無害——因為 `onFactIngested` 的 `factId` 參數宣告了但函式內根本沒用到——但哪天真的用上就會 mismatch。要嘛改取 `m[1]` 加前綴（或直接用現成的 `extractFactId()`），要嘛把這個死參數拿掉。

## 通過的部分

- `npx tsc --noEmit` 通過
- 三個 log 路徑（`hit-log.jsonl` / `events.jsonl` / `observations.jsonl`）與各寫入端一致；`facts_Topic` 目錄命名正確
- wiki index 解析的 `- [[slug]] — 描述` 格式與 `wiki-retrieval.ts` 一致
- `onFactIngested` 全程 try-catch、fire-and-forget，不會影響 `remember()` 主路徑——設計正確
- `.gitignore` 加 `output/` 沒問題

## 建議

commit 不用 revert（hook 那條線是好的、也不會壞現有功能），但 roadmap 的「✅ 全部完成」建議改成「程式碼落地、接線待補」，或直接補三件事：修 `loadTopicNames`（問題 1）、把 `evaluateForAutoSave` 接進 run-prompt 完成點、讓 `/dream` wikisync 讀 ripple log 和 auto-save 候選。需要我直接修的話說一聲。

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Critical bugs found in P0 feature commit - auto-save and ripple integration non-functional</title>
  <subtitle>Code review revealed query auto-save has parsing bug preventing all triggers, activity log is dead code, and ingest ripple lacks consumer wiring</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>query-auto-save.ts attempts to parse topics.json as JSON but file contains JSONC with // comments causing parse failures</fact>
    <fact>loadTopicNames() reads wrong field name 't.name' instead of actual schema field 'topic' from topics.json</fact>
    <fact>evaluateForAutoSave() and queryActivity functions have zero callers in codebase - not integrated into run-prompt.ts</fact>
    <fact>Ingest Ripple onFactIngested() correctly wired to mcp-memory.ts remember() but getRippleStatus() and clearProcessedRipples() have no consumers</fact>
    <fact>wiki-ripple.jsonl written by ingest ripple but never read by /dream wikisync workflow</fact>
    <fact>getShardFactIds() manually constructs topic.md filename without using topicToFilename() leading to case and whitespace mismatches</fact>
    <fact>mcp-memory.ts line 116 extracts factId with brackets [f_xxxxxx] but wiki frontmatter expects f_xxxxxx format</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Comprehensive code review of recent P0 features commit revealed three modules are not production-ready despite roadmap marking them complete. Query auto-save has fatal bug: loadTopicNames() attempts JSON.parse on topics.json which contains JSONC format with // comments that other modules (topic-rules.ts) correctly strip before parsing. Additionally reads wrong field 't.name' when schema uses 'topic'. Combined effect: topic list always empty, detectTopics always returns zero matches, never reaches MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY threshold of 3, shouldSave always false - feature cannot trigger. Activity log and auto-save evaluation functions exist but are completely unintegrated - no call sites in run-prompt.ts despite commit message claiming auto-detection of quality responses. Ingest Ripple partially wired: producer side correctly hooks into mcp-memory.ts remember() as fire-and-forget with proper error isolation, but consumer side missing - status retrieval and ripple log consumption not implemented in /dream wikisync. Additional issues: shard filename construction duplicates logic instead of reusing topicToFilename() causing mismatches on uppercase/whitespace topics, and factId extraction includes brackets creating format mismatch with wiki sources. TypeScript compilation passes and git ignore correctly configured. Recommendation: fix loadTopicNames to reuse getTopicRules(), wire evaluateForAutoSave into run-prompt completion, integrate ripple log reading into wikisync, and deduplicate filename logic.</narrative>

  <request>User requested fixes for all issues identified in P0 features code review (query auto-save, activity log, ingest ripple)</request>
  <investigated>Conducted comprehensive code review of recent commit introducing three P0 modules. Examined query-auto-save.ts, activity-log.ts, ingest-ripple.ts, and their integration points in mcp-memory.ts and run-prompt.ts. Verified TypeScript compilation status, searched codebase for function call sites, traced data flow paths, and compared implementations against existing patterns in topic-rules.ts and facts-store.ts.</investigated>
  <learned>Query auto-save has fatal JSONC parsing bug where topics.json contains // comments but loadTopicNames() uses raw JSON.parse without stripping comments like topic-rules.ts does. Also reads wrong field name causing empty topic lists and preventing feature from ever triggering. Auto-save and activity log functions are dead code with zero integration into run-prompt.ts. Ingest Ripple correctly hooks producer side into mcp-memory.ts remember() but consumer side (getRippleStatus, clearProcessedRipples, wikisync reading) completely missing. Shard filename construction duplicates logic instead of reusing canonical topicToFilename() function causing case/whitespace mismatches.</learned>
  <completed>Code review completed identifying one P0 critical bug (auto-save cannot trigger), two integration gaps (auto-save and activity log not called, ripple log not consumed), and three medium-severity issues (shard filename logic duplication, factId format mismatch, dead parameter). TypeScript compilation verified passing. Roadmap accuracy discrepancy documented.</completed>
  <next_steps>User requested "fix them all" - primary session preparing to implement fixes: rewrite loadTopicNames to reuse getTopicRules(), wire evaluateForAutoSave into run-prompt.ts completion handler, integrate ripple log consumption into /dream wikisync workflow, deduplicate shard filename logic to use topicToFilename(), and fix factId extraction format.</next_steps>
  <notes>Only one of three P0 modules (Ingest Ripple) partially functional - producer side works but consumer missing. Other two modules are completely non-functional dead code. Roadmap incorrectly marks all as complete. All issues are fixable without reverting commit since core hook architecture is sound and changes don't break existing functionality.</notes>
</summary>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>WIKI_QUERY token architecture and wikisync command location</title>
  <subtitle>Agent-initiated wiki page creation uses WIKI_QUERY token parsed by observerTransformer and handled in run-prompt with meta prompt injection</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>WIKI_QUERY token format is <<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>> parsed by observerTransformer.ts line 509</fact>
    <fact>run-prompt.ts line 1257 handles WIKI_QUERY by injecting meta prompt that writes to MEMORY_DIR/wiki/queries/{slug}.md</fact>
    <fact>WIKI_QUERY handling includes automatic index.md update with wikilink and summary line</fact>
    <fact>query-auto-save.ts designed to supplement WIKI_QUERY coverage when agent forgets to add token</fact>
    <fact>wikisync command defined in dream-config.ts line 78 with label "從 topic shards 產出/更新 wiki pages"</fact>
    <fact>observerTransformer.ts line 685 lists WIKI_QUERY in token opener regex alongside GOAL_DONE, SKILL_USED, SKILL_PROPOSE</fact>
    <fact>run-prompt.ts line 224 checks ab7.wikiQuery presence and adds to ignored list if found</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation into existing wiki page creation architecture revealed how agent-initiated saves work and where auto-save should integrate. The WIKI_QUERY token uses <<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>> format parsed by observerTransformer.ts into ab7.wikiQuery payload. When detected in run-prompt.ts (line 1257), system injects meta prompt instructing agent to write structured wiki page to queries directory following SCHEMA.md frontmatter format, with automatic index.md update and wikilink creation. The query-auto-save.ts module was explicitly designed to supplement this mechanism for cases where agent doesn't remember to add token. The wikisync command exists in dream-config.ts line 78 (not dream.ts as initially expected) and is responsible for generating wiki pages from topic shards. This establishes the integration points: evaluateForAutoSave should be called after run-prompt completion when ab7.wikiQuery is absent, and wikisync implementation should consume wiki-ripple.jsonl to incorporate pending fact ingests into wiki generation workflow.</narrative>

    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/query-auto-save.ts</file>
    <file>src/dream-config.ts</file>
    <file>src/memory.ts</file>
  </files_read>

</observation>

This reuses the SAME reply\\n// transform (transformReplyTokens) + render/split helpers as the normal turn —\\n// there is deliberately NO second transform. Tokens that only make sense inside\\n// a live turn (GOAL_DONE / RESTART / CONTINUE / WIKI_QUERY / SKILL_PROPOSE /\\n// SPECIALIST_PROXY / relay*) are intentionally ignored here; user-facing tokens\\n// (text / SEND_FILE / ASK / SCHEDULE / STICKER) are delivered.\\nexport async function flushOrphanTurn(session: ChatSession, rawText: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const { bot, rateLimitedUntil, queuePendingMessage, scheduler, scheduleStore } = deps();\\n  const chatId = session.chatId;\\n\\n  // Dedup: skip content already delivered (e.g. same summary that also came\\n  // through a normal turn, or a duplicate orphan flush). Shares the 5-min\\n  // window used by specialist/parallel result injection.\\n  if (isDuplicateResult(chatId, rawText)) {\\n    console.log(`[orphan ${chatId}] skipped (dedup hit)`);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  const ab7 = transformReplyTokens(rawText, {\\n    parseDuration,\\n    parseAbsoluteTime,\\n    parseRecurrence,\\n    computeNextRunAt,\\n  });\\n\\n  // SCHEDULE tokens — register the same way the normal path does.\\n  const scheduleReceipts: string[] = [];\\n  for (const se of ab7.scheduleTokens) {\\n    try {\\n      const entry = await scheduleStore.add({\\n        chatId,\\n        userId: session.userId ?? chatId,\\n        runAt: se.runAt,\\n        prompt: se.prompt,\\n        recurrence: se.recurrence,\\n      });\\n      scheduler.arm(entry);\\n      const delta = formatRelative(se.runAt - Date.now());\\n      const recurTag = se.recurrence ? ` [${formatRecurrence(se.recurrence)}]` : \\\"\\\";\\n      scheduleReceipts.push(\\n        `⏰ 已排程 ${entry.id}${recurTag}，${delta} 後觸發（${new Date(se.runAt).toLocaleString()}）`,\\n      );\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`[orphan ${chatId}] schedule token register failed:`, err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Surface (but don't act on) tokens that require live-turn context.\\n  const ignored: string[] = [];\\n  if (ab7.goalDone) ignored.push(\\\"GOAL_DONE\\\");\\n  if (ab7.restartToken) ignored.push(\\\"RESTART\\\");\\n  if (ab7.continueToken) ignored.push(\\\"CONTINUE\\\");\\n  if (ab7.wikiQuery) ignored.push(\\\"WIKI_QUERY\\\");\\n  if (ab7.skillPropose) ignored.push(\\\"SKILL_PROPOSE\\\");\\n  if (ab7.specialistProxy) ignored.push(\\\"SPECIALIST_PROXY\\\");\\n  if (ignored.length) {\\n    console.log(`[orphan ${chatId}] ignoring turn-context token(s): ${ignored.join(\\\", \\\")}`);\\n  }\\n\\n  const body = scheduleReceipts.length\\n    ? (ab7.text ? `${ab7.text}\\\\n\\\\n${scheduleReceipts.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\")}` : scheduleReceipts.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n    : ab7.text;\\n  const askKeyboard = buildAskKeyboard(ab7.askTokens);\\n\\n  // Text delivery (chunked). Rate-limit aware: queue for replay if throttled.\\n  if (body.trim().length > 0) {\\n    const chunks = splitForTelegram(body);\\n    for (let i = 0; i < chunks.length; i++) {\\n      const isLast = i === chunks.length - 1;\\n      const pageTag = chunks.length > 1 ? `(${i + 1}/${chunks.length})\\\\n` : \\\"\\\";\\n      const text = pageTag + chunks[i];\\n      const extra = isLast && askKeyboard ? { reply_markup: askKeyboard } : undefined;\\n      if (rateLimitedUntil() > Date.now()) {\\n        queuePendingMessage(chatId, text);\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      try {\\n        const msg = await bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, text, extra);\\n        if (isLast && askKeyboard && msg?.message_id !== undefined) {\\n          askRegistry.set(msg.message_id, ab7.askTokens);\\n        }\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.error(`[orphan ${chatId}] chunk send failed, queueing for replay:`, err);\\n        queuePendingMessage(chatId, text);\\n      }\\n      if (!isLast) await new Promise<void>((r) => setTimeout(r, 1100));\\n    }\\n  } else if (askKeyboard) {\\n    // ASK-only orphan turn (rare) — carry the keyboard on a minimal message.\\n    try {\\n      const msg = await bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, \\\"（背景任務完成）\\\", { reply_markup: askKeyboard });\\n      if (msg?.message_id !== undefined) askRegistry.set(msg.message_id, ab7.askTokens);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`[orphan ${chatId}] ask-only send failed:`, err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // SEND_FILE — sendFilesByPaths needs a Context-like; build a minimal shim.\",\"numLines\":120,\"startLine\":150,\"totalLines\":1461}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
The user is investigating the wikisync implementation. They've found:

1. The wikisync command is registered in bot-setup.ts and index.ts
2. The actual implementation is in commands/memory.ts, starting at line 245
3. The WIKISYNC_PROMPT is a meta prompt that tells the agent to:
   - Create wiki pages from topic shards (≥5 facts)
   - Follow SCHEMA.md frontmatter format
   - Extract fact IDs from shard lines [f_xxxxxx] and store in sources field
   - Update index.md with new pages
   - Handle up to 5 topics at a time

The key insight here is that wikisync is currently implemented as a meta prompt (not a dedicated function), and it currently does NOT read:
- wiki-ripple.jsonl (from ingest-ripple.ts)
- auto-save candidates (from query-auto-save.ts)

The comments in ingest-ripple.ts line 14 and query-auto-save.ts line 17 mention that "/dream wikisync step should read ripple log" and "wikisync should consume candidate list", but the actual WIKISYNC_PROMPT doesn't include those instructions yet.

This confirms the integration gap identified in the earlier review - the producer side exists but the consumer side is not wired up.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Wikisync implementation is meta prompt without ripple or auto-save integration</title>
  <subtitle>WIKISYNC_PROMPT in commands/memory.ts generates wiki pages from topic shards but lacks instructions to consume ripple log or auto-save candidates</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>handleWikiSync in commands/memory.ts line 264 invokes runPrompt with WIKISYNC_PROMPT as meta prompt</fact>
    <fact>WIKISYNC_PROMPT instructs agent to process topics with ≥5 facts and generate wiki/concepts pages with frontmatter sources field</fact>
    <fact>wikisync extracts fact IDs from shard lines using [f_xxxxxx] format and stores in frontmatter sources array</fact>
    <fact>Current wikisync logic reads topics.json, calls list_facts per topic, generates pages for shards ≥5 facts without wiki page</fact>
    <fact>WIKISYNC_PROMPT processes maximum 5 topics per run to avoid context overflow</fact>
    <fact>ingest-ripple.ts line 14 comment states wikisync step should read ripple log but WIKISYNC_PROMPT contains no such instruction</fact>
    <fact>query-auto-save.ts line 17 comment states wikisync should consume candidate list but WIKISYNC_PROMPT does not reference auto-save-candidates.jsonl</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Deep dive into wikisync implementation revealed it is a meta prompt-based command rather than procedural code, delegating all logic to agent via WIKISYNC_PROMPT instructions. The prompt directs agent to iterate through topics from topics.json, call list_facts for each, and generate wiki/concepts pages for shards with ≥5 facts that lack corresponding wiki pages. Frontmatter sources field populated by extracting [f_xxxxxx] IDs from shard content. Processing capped at 5 topics per invocation to prevent context explosion. Critical gap confirmed: despite comments in ingest-ripple.ts line 14 and query-auto-save.ts line 17 stating wikisync should consume ripple log and auto-save candidates, the actual WIKISYNC_PROMPT contains no instructions to read wiki-ripple.jsonl or auto-save-candidates.jsonl. This explains why getRippleStatus/clearProcessedRipples and getPendingCandidates/markCandidateProcessed have no callers - the consumer side integration was planned but never implemented in the prompt text.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/index.ts</file>
    <file>src/ingest-ripple.ts</file>
    <file>src/query-auto-save.ts</file>
    <file>src/dream-config.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

(ab7.text ? `${ab7.text}\\\\n\\\\n${scheduleReceipts.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\")}` : scheduleReceipts.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n    : ab7.text;\\n  const askKeyboard = buildAskKeyboard(ab7.askTokens);\\n\\n  // Text delivery (chunked). <observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>RunPrompt mode architecture determines when auto-save evaluation should trigger</title>
  <subtitle>runPrompt accepts RunPromptMode with four kinds: user, meta, continuation, relay - auto-save should only evaluate after user-kind completions</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>RunPromptMode type union defines four kinds: user, meta, continuation, relay at run-prompt.ts lines 58-61</fact>
    <fact>isMeta flag set to true when mode.kind equals "meta" at run-prompt.ts line 313</fact>
    <fact>WIKI_QUERY token handling at line 1254 injects meta prompt via runPrompt with kind: "meta"</fact>
    <fact>flushOrphanTurn function at line 175 explicitly ignores WIKI_QUERY tokens because they require live-turn context</fact>
    <fact>Parallel dispatch results inject meta prompts with kind: "meta" at lines 1099, 1211, 1214</fact>
    <fact>runPrompt mode parameter defaults to {kind: "user"} when not specified at line 306</fact>
    <fact>Meta prompts can have optional silent flag to suppress certain behaviors at line 60</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation into runPrompt architecture revealed four execution modes that determine appropriate integration point for auto-save evaluation. The RunPromptMode union type defines user (normal user messages), meta (system-injected prompts for background tasks like WIKI_QUERY processing, parallel results, or specialist responses), continuation (multi-turn goal flows), and relay (specialist proxy forwarding). The isMeta flag guards behavior that should only occur for user-initiated prompts. WIKI_QUERY handling demonstrates the pattern: when agent outputs <<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>> token during user turn, system injects meta prompt to write wiki page. The orphan turn flusher explicitly documents that WIKI_QUERY and similar tokens are ignored outside live turns because they require turn context. This establishes the integration rule: evaluateForAutoSave should only run at the END of non-meta runPrompt calls (mode.kind === "user" or "continuation"), after ab7 transform, when ab7.wikiQuery is absent (agent didn't already request wiki save). Meta-mode calls should skip evaluation to prevent infinite recursion where auto-save triggers meta prompt which triggers auto-save again.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>RunPrompt execution mode flags control meta vs user behavior branching</title>
  <subtitle>isMeta and isSilentMeta flags derived from mode parameter guard behaviors that should only occur for user-initiated prompts versus system injections</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>runPrompt function signature at line 302 accepts text and mode parameter with default {kind: "user"}</fact>
    <fact>isMeta flag set to true when mode.kind equals "meta" at line 313</fact>
    <fact>isSilentMeta flag combines meta mode check with mode.silent === true at line 314</fact>
    <fact>replyHeader only extracted for mode.kind === "user" to prepend visible reply context at line 321</fact>
    <fact>isRelay flag set when mode.kind === "relay" for specialist proxy forwarding at line 316</fact>
    <fact>continuationContext extracted only when mode.kind === "continuation" at line 312</fact>
    <fact>Session inflight guard at line 325 prevents concurrent prompts on same ACP session regardless of mode</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Detailed examination of runPrompt mode handling revealed comprehensive flag derivation pattern used throughout function to branch behavior. The mode parameter defaults to user-kind, with isMeta and isSilentMeta flags computed early (lines 313-314) to guard system-injected prompts that shouldn't trigger user-facing behaviors. The replyHeader extraction demonstrates the pattern: only user-mode prompts get visible reply headers prepended because meta/continuation/relay modes are system-internal. Continuation mode extracts context for multi-turn goal flows, relay mode identifies specialist proxy responses. Session inflight concurrency guard applies to all modes, blocking overlapping prompts regardless of origin to protect ACP session state. This flag architecture establishes where auto-save integration belongs: after agent output transform (ab7), check !isMeta && !ab7.wikiQuery, then call evaluateForAutoSave to detect quality responses that agent forgot to mark. Meta-mode prompts must skip this check to prevent recursion where auto-save triggers wiki creation which itself is a meta prompt.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Memory command handlers use deps() dependency injection pattern</title>
  <subtitle>All command handlers in commands/memory.ts import shared services via deps() function providing isAllowed, sessions, runPrompt, and other core functions</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>commands/memory.ts imports deps function from ./deps.js at line 15</fact>
    <fact>handleRemember function calls deps() to destructure isAllowed, sessions, extractFromSession services at line 20</fact>
    <fact>handleWikiSync at line 264 destructures isAllowed, sessions, runPrompt from deps()</fact>
    <fact>Memory commands import memory.js functions: appendFact, loadFacts, getFactsPath, writeFacts, listFacts at line 8</fact>
    <fact>Commands import specialist and facts-store modules for topic shard operations at lines 10-14</fact>
    <fact>MEMORY_DIR constant set from config.memoryDir at line 17 used throughout command handlers</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Examination of memory command handlers revealed the dependency injection architecture used across all command modules. The deps() function provides access to core services including authentication (isAllowed), session management (sessions), prompt execution (runPrompt), and rate limiting without direct imports, enabling testability and decoupling. Command handlers follow consistent pattern: destructure needed services from deps(), validate authorization via isAllowed(ctx), retrieve session, then execute command logic. This establishes the pattern for integrating evaluateForAutoSave into run-prompt: the function is already in the main execution path, so it can directly import from query-auto-save.ts without going through deps(), but will need access to session history and message body to pass to the evaluator. The handleWikiSync pattern at line 264 shows how to inject meta prompts back into runPrompt for triggered actions.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>

    <fact>MEMORY_DIR defaults to F:\\AI\\AIMemory when env var not set, matching config.ts logic at line 25</fact>

  <narrative>Examination of topic smoke test revealed it imports and exercises the correct topic-rules.js functions that query-auto-save.ts should be using but isn't. The script imports keywordsForTopic and classifyFact from topic-rules module which properly handle JSONC parsing of topics.json (stripping // comments before JSON.parse as seen in earlier code review). The broken loadTopicNames() in query-auto-save.ts reimplements this logic incorrectly by attempting raw JSON.parse and reading wrong field name. The fix path is clear: query-auto-save should import and call getTopicRules() from topic-rules.ts (or keywordsForTopic if only keywords needed) instead of maintaining separate buggy parsing logic. The smoke test also demonstrates proper userId detection pattern and verifies shard integrity, establishing that updated auto-save integration should be testable via similar smoke test patterns.</narrative>

from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\nimport { getTopicRules } from \\\"./topic-rules.js\\\";\",\"originalFile\":\"/**\\n * query-auto-save.ts — 自動偵測優質回覆並儲存為 Wiki Query page\\n *\\n * 補強 <<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>> token 的覆蓋率。Agent 不一定每次都記得\\n * 加 token，這個模組在回覆完成後自動評估是否值得存入 wiki。\\n *\\n * 觸發條件（全部滿足）：\\n * 1. 回覆長度 ≥ MIN_REPLY_LENGTH（200 字）\\n * 2. agent 未自行加 WIKI_QUERY token\\n * 3. 不是 meta / continuation / relay 等非使用者直接提問的回合\\n * 4. 回覆涉及 ≥ MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY 個不同 topic\\n * 5. 同一 slug 不重複儲存（dedup）\\n *\\n * 設計：\\n * - 不直接觸發 wiki 寫入（那是 agent 的工作）\\n * - 產出候選清單到 wiki-auto-save-candidates.jsonl\\n * - /dream wikisync 步驟讀取候選清單，由 agent 一次性處理\\n * - 可選：即時觸發 meta prompt（同 WIKI_QUERY 機制，由呼叫端決定）\\n */\\n\\nimport { readFileSync, existsSync, appendFileSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── 常數 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** 回覆最低字數（含 CJK） */\\nconst MIN_REPLY_LENGTH = 200;\\n\\n/** 最少涉及幾個不同 topic 才算「綜合性回覆」 */\\nconst MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY = 3;\\n\\n/** 候選 log 最大筆數 */\\nconst MAX_CANDIDATES = 100;\\n\\n/** slug 最大長度 */\\nconst MAX_SLUG_LENGTH = 80;\\n\\n/** 候選 log 路徑 */\\nconst CANDIDATES_PATH = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"wiki\\\", \\\"wiki-auto-save-candidates.jsonl\\\");\\n\\n/** 既有 wiki queries 目錄 */\\nconst WIKI_QUERIES_DIR = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"wiki\\\", \\\"queries\\\");\\n\\n// ─── 型別 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface AutoSaveCandidate {\\n  /** ISO timestamp */\\n  ts: string;\\n  /** 建議的 wiki slug */\\n  slug: string;\\n  /** 自動產出的摘要（≤200 字） */\\n  summary: string;\\n  /** 匹配到的 topics */\\n  matchedTopics: string[];\\n  /** 回覆長度 */\\n  replyLength: number;\\n  /** 使用者原始問題（截斷） */\\n  questionPreview: string;\\n  /** 是否已被處理 */\\n  processed: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface AutoSaveEvalResult {\\n  /** 是否值得儲存 */\\n  shouldSave: boolean;\\n  /** 建議的 slug */\\n  slug: string | null;\\n  /** 建議的 summary */\\n  summary: string | null;\\n  /** 匹配到的 topics */\\n  matchedTopics: string[];\\n  /** 跳過原因（如果不儲存） */\\n  skipReason: string | null;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── 內部工具 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** 載入 topics.json 的 topic 名稱列表 */\\nfunction loadTopicNames(): string[] {\\n  const topicsPath = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"topics.json\\\");\\n  if (!existsSync(topicsPath)) return [];\\n  try {\\n    const raw = JSON.parse(readFileSync(topicsPath, \\\"utf8\\\"));\\n    if (Array.isArray(raw)) {\\n      return string, topicNames: string[]): string[] {\\n  const lower = text.toLowerCase();\\n  const matched: string[] = [];\\n  for (const topic of topicNames) {\\n    // topic 名是 kebab-case，轉成可搜尋的關鍵字\\n content.thinking: The user is editing query-auto-save.ts to add an import for getTopicRules from topic-rules.js. This is the fix for the first critical bug identified in the code review - the broken loadTopicNames() function that tries to parse JSONC as JSON.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Import getTopicRules to fix JSONC parsing in query-auto-save</title>
  <subtitle>Added import from topic-rules.js to enable proper topics.json parsing with comment stripping instead of broken raw JSON.parse</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added import statement for getTopicRules from ./topic-rules.js to query-auto-save.ts line 24</fact>
    <fact>This prepares fix for loadTopicNames() function which currently fails on JSONC comments in topics.json</fact>
    <fact>topic-rules.js getTopicRules() properly strips // comments before parsing JSON unlike current broken implementation</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>First fix applied to query-auto-save.ts critical parsing bug. Added import for getTopicRules function from topic-rules module which provides correct JSONC parsing logic (strips // comments before JSON.parse as seen in topic-rules.ts lines 119-126). This enables replacement of broken loadTopicNames() function that attempts raw JSON.parse on topics.json containing // comment headers, causing parse exceptions and returning empty array. The empty topic list causes detectTopics to match zero topics, never reaching MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY threshold of 3, making shouldSave permanently false. Next step will be rewriting loadTopicNames to call getTopicRules instead of reimplementing buggy parsing logic.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/query-auto-save.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
This is a significant fix - the user has replaced the broken loadTopicNames() and detectTopics() functions with a corrected version that uses getTopicRules() from topic-rules.js. Let me document what changed:

1. Removed the loadTopicNames() function entirely (which was doing raw JSON.parse and reading wrong field name)
2. Rewrote detectTopics() to:
   - Remove the topicNames parameter (no longer needed)
   - Call getTopicRules() directly to get properly parsed topic rules
   - Use both rule.topic split words AND rule.keywords for matching (more accurate)
   - Add lowercase normalization for keyword matching
   - Add length check (kw.length >= 2) for keywords

This fixes the P0 bug where topics.json JSONC comments caused parse failures and wrong field name caused empty topic lists.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Replaced broken loadTopicNames and detectTopics with getTopicRules-based implementation</title>
  <subtitle>Eliminated JSONC parsing bug and field name mismatch by calling topic-rules.js getTopicRules instead of reimplementing broken parsing logic</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Removed loadTopicNames() function that failed on JSONC // comments and read wrong field name t.name instead of topic</fact>
    <fact>Rewrote detectTopics() to call getTopicRules() directly instead of taking topicNames parameter</fact>
    <fact>Enhanced topic matching to use both rule.topic split words and rule.keywords array for improved accuracy</fact>
    <fact>Added lowercase normalization for keyword matching: kw.toLowerCase() comparison at line 91</fact>
    <fact>Added length check kw.length >= 2 to filter out single-character matches</fact>
    <fact>getTopicRules() provides JSONC comment stripping, schema validation, and caching that broken implementation lacked</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Core bugfix applied to query-auto-save.ts resolving the fatal parsing issue that prevented auto-save feature from ever triggering. The broken loadTopicNames() function attempted raw JSON.parse on topics.json containing JSONC // comment headers (causing parse exceptions) and read wrong field name 't.name' when actual schema uses 'topic'. This caused topics list to always be empty, detectTopics to match zero topics, never reaching MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY threshold of 3, making shouldSave permanently false. The fix eliminates duplicate parsing logic by calling getTopicRules() from topic-rules.js which properly strips JSONC comments (lines 119-126 in topic-rules.ts), validates schema, and provides caching. Enhanced detectTopics signature removes topicNames parameter since getTopicRules() is called directly, and improved matching logic uses both topic name split on dashes and explicit keywords array from topic rules, providing more accurate detection than guessing keywords from kebab-case names. Added proper lowercase normalization and minimum keyword length check to reduce false positives. This change eliminates the P0 critical bug making auto-save functional.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/query-auto-save.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

// 至少一個關鍵字出現在文中\\n    if (needles.some((kw) => kw.length >= 2 && lower.includes(kw.toLowerCase()))) {\\n      matched.push(rule.topic);\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return matched;\\n}\\n\\n/** 從使用者問題和回覆內容產出 (/[\\\\u4e00-\\\\u9fff]/.test(match)) return \\\"-\\\";\\n      return \\\"-\\\";\\n    })\\n    .replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \\\"-\\\")\\n    .replace(/-+/g, \\\"-\\\")\\n    .replace(/^-|-$/g, \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  // 如果 slug 太短或全是 dash（中文問題），加時間戳\\n  if (slug.length < 3) {\\n    slug = `query-${Date.now().toString(36)}`;\\n  }\\n\\n  return slug.slice(0, MAX_SLUG_LENGTH);\\n}\\n\\n/** */\\nfunction slugExists(slug: string): boolean {\\n  // 檢查 wiki/queries/ 目錄\\n  if (existsSync(path.join(WIKI_QUERIES_DIR, `${slug}.md`))) return true;\\n\\n  // 檢查候選 log 中是否已有此 slug\\n  if return false; }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── 公開 API ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/**\\n * 評估一個回覆是否值得自動存入 wiki。\\n * 呼叫時機：run-prompt 完成後、非 meta 模式、agent 未自行加 WIKI_QUERY token。\\n *\\n * @param question - 使用者原始問題\\n * @param reply - agent 完整回覆文字\\n * @returns 評估結果\\n */\\nexport function evaluateForAutoSave(question: string, reply: string): AutoSaveEvalResult {\\n  const result: AutoSaveEvalResult = {\\n    shouldSave: false,\\n    slug: null,\\n    summary: null,\\n    matchedTopics: [],\\n    skipReason: null,\\n  };\\n\\n  // 條件 1：長度門檻\\n  if (reply.length < MIN_REPLY_LENGTH) {\\n    result.skipReason = `回覆太短（${reply.length} < ${MIN_REPLY_LENGTH}）`;\\n    return result;\\n  }\\n\\n  // 條件 2：topic 多樣性\\n  const topicNames = loadTopicNames();\\n  const matched = detectTopics(reply, topicNames);\\n  result.matchedTopics = matched;\\n\\n  if (matched.length < MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY) {\\n    result.skipReason = `topic 多樣性不足（${matched.length} < ${MIN_TOPIC_DIVERSITY}）`;\\n    return result;\\n  }\\n\\n  // 產出 slug 和 summary\\n  const slug = generateSlug(question);\\n  const summary = generateSummary(question, reply);\\n\\n  // 條件 3：dedup\\n  if (slugExists(slug)) {\\n    result.skipReason = `slug \\\"${slug}\\\" 已存在`;\\n    return result;\\n  }\\n\\n  result.shouldSave = true;\\n  result.slug = slug;\\n  result.summary = summary;\\n  return result;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * 把一個通過評估的候選寫入候選 log。\\n * 呼叫端可決定是否同時觸發 meta prompt（即時寫 wiki page）。\\n */\\nexport function recordAutoSaveCandidate(\\n  question: string,\\n  reply: string,\\n  evalResult: AutoSaveEvalResult,\\n): AutoSaveCandidate | null {\\n  if (!evalResult.shouldSave || !evalResult.slug || !evalResult.summary) return null;\\n\\n  const candidate: AutoSaveCandidate = {\\n    ts: new Date().toISOString(),\\n    slug: evalResult.slug,\\n    summary: evalResult.summary,\\n    matchedTopics: evalResult.matchedTopics,\\n    replyLength: reply.length,\\n    questionPreview: question.slice(0, 100),\\n    processed: false,\\n  };\\n\\n  try {\\n    const dir = path.dirname(CANDIDATES_PATH);\\n    if (!existsSync(dir)) mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });\\n    appendFileSync(CANDIDATES_PATH, JSON.stringify(candidate) + \\\"\\\\n\\\", \\\"utf8\\\");\\n\\n    // 體積控制\\n    trimCandidates();\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.error(\\\"[query-auto-save] failed content.thinking:
The user is fixing the issues identified in the code review. Now they're adding an import for `topicToFilename` from `facts-store.js` to the `ingest-ripple.ts` file. This is to fix the issue where `getShardFactIds()` manually constructs the shard filename instead of using the canonical filename conversion function.

This addresses the medium-severity problem #4 from the code review: "shard 檔名是用猜的" - where `getShardFactIds` self-constructed `${topic}.md` without reusing the official `topicToFilename()` function that properly handles lowercase conversion, space-to-dash, and special character filtering.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Import topicToFilename to fix shard filename construction in ingest-ripple</title>
  <subtitle>Added import from facts-store.js to enable canonical topic-to-filename conversion instead of manual kebab-case guessing</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added import statement for topicToFilename from ./facts-store.js to ingest-ripple.ts line 20</fact>
    <fact>Prepares fix for getShardFactIds() which manually constructs topic.md filenames at lines 134-137</fact>
    <fact>topicToFilename() in facts-store.ts line 59 provides canonical conversion: lowercase, space-to-dash, special character filtering</fact>
    <fact>Manual construction fails on topics with uppercase letters or spaces causing pendingCount calculation errors</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Second bugfix preparation addressing medium-severity issue in ingest-ripple.ts shard filename construction. The getShardFactIds() function currently self-constructs filenames as `${topic}.md` without using the canonical topicToFilename() function from facts-store.ts (line 59) which properly handles lowercase conversion, space-to-dash transformation, and special character filtering. This causes mismatches when topic names contain uppercase letters or spaces - the function fails to locate existing shard files, calculates pendingCount as 0, and ripple threshold never triggers. The import enables next step: rewriting getShardFactIds to call topicToFilename(topic) instead of string interpolation, ensuring consistent filename resolution across all modules that access topic shards.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/ingest-ripple.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Replace manual shard filename construction with canonical topicToFilename call</title>
  <subtitle>Eliminated duplicate logic and case/whitespace bugs by using facts-store topicToFilename instead of guessing topic.md filenames</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Removed manual filename candidates array with two guessed patterns at ingest-ripple.ts lines 134-137</fact>
    <fact>Replaced with single topicToFilename(topic) call that applies canonical normalization</fact>
    <fact>topicToFilename provides lowercase conversion, space-to-dash, special character filtering matching write-side logic</fact>
    <fact>Old code tried ${topic}.md and ${topic.replace(/-/g, "_")}.md patterns causing mismatches on uppercase or space topics</fact>
    <fact>New implementation guarantees filename matches actual shard files created by facts-store write operations</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Applied bugfix eliminating duplicate filename construction logic in getShardFactIds. The old implementation maintained two manual filename patterns trying to guess shard filenames, but lacked the normalization rules used by facts-store.ts when actually writing shards. This caused pendingCount calculation failures when topic names contained uppercase letters or spaces - the function couldn't locate existing shard files, returned empty fact ID arrays, calculated pendingCount as 0, and ripple threshold never triggered even when legitimate pending facts existed. The fix replaces candidates array with single call to topicToFilename(topic) from facts-store module, ensuring exact same lowercase + space-to-dash + special-char-filtering normalization used by write side. Comment added explaining the rationale: reuse canonical converter from facts-store to avoid filename guess misses. This deduplicates logic and fixes the medium-severity bug where case/whitespace mismatches prevented ripple detection.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/ingest-ripple.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
The primary session is now adding factId to the RippleEntry interface and populating it in the onFactIngested function. This addresses the medium-severity issue mentioned in the code review where factId parameter was declared but never used. Now it's being captured in the ripple entry for traceability.

These edits show:
1. Added factId field to RippleEntry interface (line 49-50)
2. Populated factId in the entry object construction (line 202)

This is a minor enhancement to capture the factId that was already being passed to onFactIngested but never recorded.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Capture factId in ripple entries for traceability</title>
  <subtitle>Added factId field to RippleEntry interface and populated it in onFactIngested to record which fact triggered each ripple event</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added factId field to RippleEntry interface at line 49-50 as nullable string in f_xxxxxx format</fact>
    <fact>Populated factId in entry object at line 202 using factId ?? null to handle optional parameter</fact>
    <fact>onFactIngested already accepted factId parameter but previously never used it</fact>
    <fact>factId enables traceability linking ripple log entries back to specific facts that triggered updates</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Minor enhancement addressing unused parameter issue in onFactIngested. The function signature already accepted optional factId parameter but never recorded it in the ripple entry, wasting traceability information. Added factId field to RippleEntry interface as nullable string (f_xxxxxx format) and populated it in entry construction using factId ?? null to handle optional parameter. This enables future debugging and auditing by linking ripple log entries back to the specific facts that triggered wiki update thresholds. While not fixing a bug per se, this eliminates dead parameter and provides useful metadata for wikisync consumption. The field is marked nullable since older calling code might not pass factId.</narrative>

  </concepts>

  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/ingest-ripple.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

process.env.MEMORY_DIR\\n    : \\\"F:\\\\\\\\AI\\\\\\\\AIMemory\\\"\\n);\\n\\nfunction activeUserId(): number {\\n  // Primary: TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID — set automatically by the telegram-kiro\\n  // bridge when it spawns the agent. Accept the shorter alias\\n  // MEMORY_USER_ID for standalone use (e.g. Codex CLI where the bridge\\n  // doesn't inject env, and the user statically configures the MCP\\n  // server via ~/.codex/config.toml [mcp_servers.memory.env]).\\n  // Finally, fall back to reading <MEMORY_DIR>/.user-id so the user can\\n  // drop a single-line file there instead of editing TOML.\\n  const envRaw =\\n    (process.env.TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID ?? \\\"\\\").trim() ||\\n    (process.env.MEMORY_USER_ID ?? \\\"\\\").trim();\\n  if (envRaw) {\\n    const n = Number(envRaw);\\n    if (Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0) return n;\\n  }\\n  try {\\n    const filePath = path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\".user-id\\\");\\n    const raw = readFileSync(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\").trim();\\n    const n = Number(raw);\\n    if (Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0) return n;\\n  } catch {\\n    // ignore — fall through to error\\n  }\\n  throw new Error(\\n    \\\"memory MCP: active user id not configured. Set one of:\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"  - env TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID (how the telegram-kiro bridge normally injects it), or\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"  - env MEMORY_USER_ID (set in ~/.codex/config.toml [mcp_servers.memory.env] for standalone Codex use), or\\\\n\\\" +\\n      `  - a plain-text file ${path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\".user-id\\\")} containing just the Telegram user id.`\\n  );\\n}\\n\\nfunction factsPath(userId: number): string {\\n  return path.join(MEMORY_DIR, `facts-${userId}.md`);\\n}\\n\\nasync function remember(text: string, topic?: string): Promise<string> {\\n  const line = text.trim();\\n  if (!line) throw new Error(\\\"text is empty\\\");\\n  const userId = activeUserId();\\n  await mkdir(MEMORY_DIR, { recursive: true });\\n\\n  // Dedup check: precise content hash (SQLite) then Jaccard fallback.\\n  const targetTopic = topic?.trim() || (await classifyFactAsync(line)) || \\\"misc\\\";\\n  if (factExists(line)) {\\n    return `⚠️ Exact duplicate detected (content hash match). Not appended.`;\\n  }\\n  const existingFacts = readTopicFacts(userId, targetTopic);\\n  const dupWarning = checkDuplicate(line, existingFacts);\\n\\n  const formatted = formatFactLine(line);\\n  await appendFile(factsPath(userId), formatted, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  // Mirror to topic shard. If caller supplied an explicit topic we trust\\n  // it; otherwise classifyFact infers from the text.\\n  const shard = appendFactShardOnly({\\n    userId,\\n    formattedLine: formatted,\\n    rawText: line,\\n    topic: topic?.trim() || undefined,\\n  });\\n  // Mirror to SQLite index\\n  const createdAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n  insertFact(line, targetTopic, createdAt);\\n  // Ripple: fire-and-forget wiki 更新偵測\\n  const factId = formatted.match(/\\\\[f_([0-9a-f]{6})\\\\]/)?.[0] ?? undefined;\\n  onFactIngested(targetTopic, line, factId);\\n  const shardNote = shard ? `, shard=${path.basename(shard.file)}` : \\\"\\\";\\n  const warn = dupWarning ? `\\\\n⚠️ Possible duplicate in shard \\\"${targetTopic}\\\": \\\"${dupWarning}\\\"` : \\\"\\\";\\n  return `ok: appended to ${factsPath(userId)}${shardNote}${warn}`;\\n}\\n\\nfunction stripFactPrefix(s: string): string {\\n  return s.replace(/^\\\\s*-\\\\s*(?:\\\\[f_[0-9a-f]{6}\\\\]\\\\s*)?\\\\[[^\\\\]]+\\\\]\\\\s*/, \\\"\\\").trim();\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Check if a new fact is semantically similar to existing facts using\\n * Jaccard word-set similarity. Returns the most similar existing fact\\n * text if similarity > 0.7, or null if no duplicate found.\\n */\\nfunction checkDuplicate(newFact: string, existingFacts: string[]): string | null {\\n  if (existingFacts.length === 0) return null;\\n  const newWords = wordSet(newFact);\\n  if (newWords.size < 3) return null; // too short to meaningfully compare\\n  let bestScore = 0;\\n  let bestFact = \\\"\\\";\\n  for (const existing of existingFacts) {\\n    const existWords = wordSet(existing);\\n    const intersection = new Set([...newWords].filter((w) => existWords.has(w)));\\n    const union = new Set([...newWords, ...existWords]);\\n    const jaccard = union.size > 0 ? intersection.size / union.size : 0;\\n    if (jaccard > bestScore) {\\n      bestScore = jaccard;\\n      bestFact = existing;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return bestScore >= 0.7 ? bestFact : null;\\n}\\n\\nfunction wordSet(text: string): Set<string> {\\n  return new Set(\\n    text.toLowerCase().replace(/[^\\\\w\\\\u4e00-\\\\u9fff]+/g, \\\" \\\").trim().split(/\\\\s+/).filter(Boolean)\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Lightweight access tracking for the decay mechanism.\\n *  Appends one JSONL line per query to fact-access-log.jsonl.\\n *  Fire-and-forget — failures are silently ignored. */\\nfunction trackAccess(query: string, hitCount: number): void {\\n  try {\\n    const logPath = path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\"fact-access-log.jsonl\\\");\\n    const entry = JSON.stringify({ ts: new Date().toISOString(), q: query, hits: hitCount }) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    appendFile(logPath, entry, \\\"utf8\\\").catch(() => {});\\n  } catch { /* best-effort */ }\\n}\\n\\nasync function listFacts(opts: { tail?: number; query?: string; explicitTail?: boolean } = {}): Promise<{\\n  facts: string[];\\n  matched: number;\\n  total: number;\\n  matchedTopic: string | null;\\n  viaShard: boolean;\\n}> {\\n  const userId = activeUserId();\\n  // Lazily load master log contents. Most topic queries skip this entirely\\n  // thanks to the shard fast-path.\\n  let masterLines: string[] | null = null;\\n  const loadMaster = async (): Promise<string[]> => {\\n    if (masterLines) return masterLines;\\n    try {\\n      const raw = await readFile(factsPath(userId), \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      masterLines = raw\\n        .split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)\\n        .map(stripFactPrefix)\\n        .filter((s) => s.length > 0);\\n    } catch {\\n      masterLines = [];\\n    }\\n    return masterLines;\\n  };\\n\\n  let facts: string[] = [];\\n  let matchedTopic: string | null = null;\\n  let viaShard = false;\\n\\n  const q = opts.query?.trim();\\n  if (q) {\\n    // Topic-name fast path: read just the one shard file. Master log is\\n    // still consulted for the `total` count so callers can see how many\\n    // facts exist overall.\\n    const kws = keywordsForTopic(q);\\n    if (kws && kws.length > 0) {\\n      matchedTopic = q.toLowerCase();\\n      facts = readTopicFacts(userId, q);\\n      viaShard = true;\\n      // If the shard is empty but keywords would still match the master\\n      // log (e.g. shard not rebuilt yet after a topics.json change), fall\\n      // back to scanning master so the agent never sees a false empty.\\n      if (facts.length === 0) {\\n        const all = await loadMaster();\\n        const lowered = kws.map((k) => k.toLowerCase());\\n        facts = all.filter((s) => {\\n          const sl = s.toLowerCase();\\n          return lowered.some((k) => sl.includes(k));\\n        });\\n        viaShard = false;\\n      }\\n    } else {\\n      // Plain substring — always scan master log.\\n      const all = await loadMaster();\\n      const ql = q.toLowerCase();\\n      facts = all.filter((s) => s.toLowerCase().includes(ql));\\n    }\\n  } else {\\n    // No query → return master log.\\n    facts = await loadMaster();\\n  }\\n\\n  const all = await loadMaster();\\n  const total = all.length;\\n  // Number of facts that matched the query *before* any tail cap. For no-\\n  // query calls this equals total. Handler uses this to decide whether\\n  // the \\\"(N more not shown)\\\" hint is honest — it's only honest when tail\\n  // actually truncated matched results, not when the master log simply\\n  // contains unrelated lines.\\n  const matched = facts.length;\\n\\n  // --- Decay tracking: log topic access for the decay mechanism ---\\n  if (q && facts.length > 0) {\\n    trackAccess(matchedTopic ?? q, facts.length);\\n  }\\n\\n  const tail =\\n    Number.isFinite(opts.tail) && (opts.tail as number) > 0\\n      ? Math.floor(opts.tail as number)\\n      : undefined;\\n  // Tail cap only applies to master-log substring scans, not to topic\\n  // shard fast-path reads. Shards are already small (typical <25 facts);\\n  // truncating at the 20-row default just forces the agent to follow up\\n  // with tail=0 every time. Master scans can still match dozens of rows\\n  // for broad queries, so the default safety net stays there.\\n  // Callers that want to narrow a shard read can still pass explicit\\n  // tail via opts.explicitTail=true.\\n  const applyTail = tail && (!viaShard || opts.explicitTail === true);\\n  if (applyTail && facts.length > (tail as number)) facts = facts.slice(-(tail as number));\\n  return { facts, matched, total, matchedTopic, viaShard };\\n}\\n\\nconst server = new Server(\\n  { name: \\\"tg-memory\\\", version: \\\"0.1.0\\\" },\\n  { capabilities: { tools: {} } }\\n);\\n\\nserver.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => ({\\n  tools: [\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"remember\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Append a single fact line to the user's long-term memory. Writes to the master log facts-<userId>.md AND mirrors to a per-topic shard facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md. Pass `topic` to force the shard (must match a name in topics.json); omit it to let the server classify via the same rules used for the preamble topic index. Use this to store stable user preferences, decisions, or project facts that should survive across chat sessions. One fact per call; short, self-contained sentences work best.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {\\n          text: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description: \\\"The fact to remember (a single short sentence).\\\",\\n          },\\n          topic: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Optional: explicit topic name (see preamble 'Memory topics'). When omitted, the server auto-classifies using topics.json.\\\",\\n          },\\n        },\\n        required: [\\\"text\\\"],\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"list_facts\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Return stored long-term memory facts for this user (timestamps stripped). Preferred usage: pass `query` = a topic name from the preamble topic index (e.g. 'bridge-token', 'scheduler') — the server reads that shard file directly AND returns the entire shard regardless of `tail` (shards are already small classified sets). If you want to narrow a shard read, pass an explicit `tail` in the call. For any other `query` string the server falls back to a case-insensitive substring scan over the master log, where `tail` (default 20) caps the result. Pass `tail=0` to disable the cap on a master scan.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {\\n          tail: {\\n            type: \\\"number\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Cap on results. For master-log substring scans, default 20 (pass 0 to return all matches). For topic-shard reads (when query matches a topic name), the cap is ignored unless you pass an explicit number — the full shard is returned by default.\\\",\\n          },\\n          query: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Topic name from the preamble index (expands to keyword OR-match) or any case-insensitive substring.\\\",\\n          },\\n        },\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"forget\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Delete facts from the user's long-term memory by case-insensitive substring match on the master log. Two-phase flow: 1) call with `query` only — if 0 matches returns an error, if exactly 1 match it is deleted immediately, if ≥2 matches it returns the candidates plus a `confirm` token and deletes nothing; 2) call again with the same `query` plus `confirm` set to that token to actually delete all matches. Any change to the master log between preview and confirm invalidates the token (you'll need to re-preview). Every delete is mirrored to all topic shards (via rebuild) and logged to forget-log.md for audit. Use this to clean up test/smoke facts or to remove stale notes — never delete silently without showing the user what's going.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {\\n          query: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Case-insensitive substring matched against each fact's text (timestamp/bullet stripped). Short distinctive phrases work best — e.g. 'facts-smoke-test' rather than a single common word.\\\",\\n          },\\n          confirm: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Confirmation token returned by the first call when multiple matches are found. Pass it back unchanged to commit the delete. Omit on the first call.\\\",\\n          },\\n        },\\n        required: [\\\"query\\\"],\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"propose_topics\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Read-only snapshot for a /topic-review pass. Returns the full list of facts (oldest first), the currently-active topics.json, per-topic shard counts, and the max-topics limit. No writes, no side effects. Use this to decide whether the current topic set still fits the master log. After you decide on a new list, call `apply_topics` with the proposed `topics` array to commit. Never call `apply_topics` without first calling `propose_topics` in the same turn — you need the fresh snapshot to make a sensible proposal.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {},\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"apply_topics\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Overwrite topics.json with a new topic list and rebuild all shards from the master log. Input `topics` must be a non-empty array of { topic: string, keywords: string[] } with unique lowercase topic names (a-z, 0-9, _, -) and ≤50 entries by default (configurable via MEMORY_MAX_TOPICS). The server validates, snapshots the old file to topics.bak.<ts>.json, writes the new file, invalidates the in-process cache, and rebuilds every topic shard so existing facts are re-classified immediately. Returns the new shard histogram so you can show the user what moved. Optionally pass `expectedToken` (from a previous call) to guard against racing edits — the server recomputes the token from the normalised proposal and rejects the call on mismatch.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {\\n          topics: {\\n            type: \\\"array\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Proposed topic list. Each entry: { topic: string (slug, ≤40 chars), keywords: string[] (≥1 non-empty substring) }. Order matters — first-match-wins during classification, so put more-specific topics before broader ones.\\\",\\n            items: {\\n              type: \\\"object\\\",\\n              properties: {\\n                topic: { type: \\\"string\\\" },\\n                keywords: {\\n                  type: \\\"array\\\",\\n                  items: { type: \\\"string\\\" },\\n                },\\n              },\\n              required: [\\\"topic\\\", \\\"keywords\\\"],\\n            },\\n          },\\n          expectedToken: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Optional. If provided, must equal computeTopicsToken(normalised proposal). After reviewing summaries, call ingest_save with your tree JSON.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {\\n          source: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description: \\\"URL (http/https) or absolute file path to ingest.\\\",\\n          },\\n          slug: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Short identifier for this index (a-z, 0-9, hyphens). Auto-generated from source if omitted.\\\",\\n          },\\n        },\\n        required: [\\\"source\\\"],\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"ingest_save\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Save the tree index JSON you built from ingest_prepare summaries. The tree should have { summary, children: [...] } with chunk_ids at leaf nodes referencing chunk indices from ingest_prepare.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {\\n          slug: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description: \\\"Must match the slug returned by ingest_prepare.\\\",\\n          },\\n          title: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description: \\\"Human-readable title for this document index.\\\",\\n          },\\n          tree: {\\n            type: \\\"object\\\",\\n            description:\\n              \\\"Hierarchical tree: { summary: string, children: [{ summary, children|chunk_ids }] }. Leaf nodes must have chunk_ids: number[] pointing to chunk indices.\\\",\\n          },\\n        },\\n        required: [\\\"slug\\\", \\\"tree\\\"],\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"list_indexes\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"List all document tree indexes available in the indexes directory. Use this to see what documents have been ingested for tree-based retrieval.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {},\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"query_index\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Query a document tree index by question. Navigates the tree using embedding similarity (or keyword fallback) and returns the most relevant chunks. Much faster than manually reading the index JSON.\\\",\\n      inputSchema: {\\n        type: \\\"object\\\",\\n        properties: {\\n          slug: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description: \\\"Index slug to query (from list_indexes).\\\",\\n          },\\n          question: {\\n            type: \\\"string\\\",\\n            description: \\\"The question to find relevant chunks for.\\\",\\n          },\\n          max_chunks: {\\n            type: \\\"number\\\",\\n            description: \\\"Max chunks to return (default 3).\\\",\\n          },\\n        },\\n        required: [\\\"slug\\\", \\\"question\\\"],\\n      },\\n    },\\n    {\\n      name: \\\"ingest_update\\\",\\n      description:\\n        \\\"Incrementally update an existing index by re-reading its source.
